calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Marina Tsalis, Esq. 650-493-9300 Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati for Petitioner.
Michelle Landry, Esq. 415-749-9526 Vedder Price for Respondent. (In-person)

The Court calls Line 1 - CPF-25-519516 Sifive v Kumar and Line 2 CPF-26-519544 Kumar v Sifive together. Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling on both lines. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received on both lines.

ARGUED.
Ms. Tsalis states both sides seeking same relief, agrees with the TR, there was an offset, tendered check.
Ms. Landry contests and presents arguments; Petition is amended by Mr. Kumar, entitled to interest.
Court comments and poses questions; hears the petition on the notice, copy of amendment not received, this does not foreclose anything.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on Line 1 - CPF-25-519516 Sifive v Kumar and Line 2 CPF-26-519544 Kumar v Sifive. Parties are orderes to meet and confer re interest issue.

Petitioner SiFive, Inc.'s unopposed Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrator's Award is granted and the 12/12/2025 Arbitration Award is confirmed in its entirety. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.) Judgment shall be entered consistent with the 12/12/2025 Award. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1287.4.)

Kumar's request for fees and costs is denied. The court finds this proceeding to confirm the award is not properly regarded as "an action based on a violation" of the relevant chapter of the Business and Professions Code. (See Business and Professions Code section 16600.5(e)(2).) Further the court finds Kumar incurred no reasonable attorney's fees or costs "respon[ding]" to Petitioner's petition, given the nature of the proceeding and the agreement on the only relief available under the Code of Civil Procedure.

Petitioner is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and a simple, straightforward proposed judgment (ideally, on the Judicial Council form and attaching the arbitrator's award) and email both to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed. The proposed judgment submitted prior to the hearing is rejected. Petitioner to resubmit via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org by 4pm today. If unable to do so, Petitioner to drop a hard copy of the proposed judgment in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Continued To Jun-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SIFIVE, INC. Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

Continued to June 18, 2026. All parties to provide courtesy copies of their filings with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date per SF Local Rule 2.7B.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
